reviews.review_msg: He provides powepoint slides. His lectures although really interesting in my opinion, arn't neccesarily what will be on the exam. Stick to the power points and I urge you to go to tutorials and do your readings because that is where most of the test material comes from.  His exams are really fair if you know the readings & the slides. Go 2 TUTORIAL
One of the most disorganized prof I've ever had. No slides and he jumps from 1 topic to the next. Exams were fair but you have to memorize all the readings. Stay away!
Garth was the best professor I ever had. I am forever grateful for having the opportunity to have him as my Professor! RIP!

genuinely cannot put into words how great of a professor Garth was. i looked forward to coming to class every week because he had a way of making such hard topics fun (for a lack of better words) to learn about. it still doesn't feel real :(  rip king. thank you for everything. legends are never forgotten.

Stay far away from CRIM 413 online if you care about your gpa. Hours of readings every week which can be very dense at times. Weekly discussion posts also take hours. Assignments are graded incredibly vaguely and have no rubric. Cherry on top is a closed book final based on 12 weeks of readings and videos where you write 4 essays in 3 hours.

Garth is an amazing prof, I had him for crim 413 with TA Mackenzie. Garth has interesting lectures, the content is fascinating, and the assignments I enjoyed doing. The class has a midterm and final, if you study and put in the time is not bad. I get reviews that are 50/50, but if you are interested in national security the class is awesome.

Mackenzie took over our terrorism course under very difficult circumstances. In doing so, she was graceful and compassionate. She held extra office hours for students to prepare for the exam. She was also very passionate about the topic!
